Pythia-12B.note: base model, cannot do multi-needle QA

Pythia-6.9B.note: base model, cannot do multi-needle QA

finding: Needle retrieval failure under naive4 requires BOTH: (1) high outlier ratio (>8x) AND (2) sufficient model instruction-following capability. Only Qwen2-7B meets both conditions. Qwen2.5-14B (low outlier, 3.5x) passes all needles even with naive4 -- confirming that the outlier ratio, not model size, determines quantization vulnerability.
threshold: Outlier ratio ~8x appears to be the critical threshold for catastrophic needle loss.
